extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. The only transition that sets sent := true is [t_send_allowed] on send_attempt, which is guarded by "not opted_out" — so sent can only become true while opted_out is false. The only transition that sets opted_out := true is [t_optout], and it simultaneously sets sent := false; thus immediately after opt-out, sent is false. There is no transition anywhere that sets opted_out back to false, so once opted_out is true it stays true forever, permanently disabling [t_send_allowed]. While opted_out is true, every available transition either sets sent := false ([t_send_blocked], [t_optout] again, and all key_* stage transitions) or leaves it unchanged — none set sent := true. The combination-lock stage machinery (key_0/key_1/key_2 advancing stages 0..12, including adv_11 reaching the final stage 12) only ever sets sent := false on every adv_* and rst_* transition; there is no backdoor at any stage that produces a send, so the lock is irrelevant to the send property. Hence no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true. File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs_hard/_judge_prompts/lock-len12-hold-105.txt
